given: Multiple stock records exist for different locations
then: Records are organized with rows grouped by location (all records for one location together before moving to the next location)
architectural_notes: Grouping/ordering by location is a deterministic-listing concern owned by the service (app/services/), which requests a stable order (e.g. ORDER BY location, sku) realized by the repository query (app/repositories/); the boundary/template only renders the already-ordered rows. E2E: asserts grouped row order in rendered UI against multi-location seeded DB state. Ordering must never live in the template.

independence.rationale: Specifies how table rows are organized by location, distinct from AC1 which only requires that a table with data exists; rows could exist in any order without violating AC1.
